title: noronhia latifolia
intro: Noronhia latifolia, also known as the Madagascar ironwood or devil's back, is a tropical evergreen tree native to the island of Madagascar, belonging to the family Ochnaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Noronhia latifolia are said to include relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to treat skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant's bark, leaves, and roots are often used in traditional remedies, and it can be consumed as a tea, infusion, or tincture.
